title: Necessity vs. Obligation
description: Distinguish must, have to, need to, mustn't, and don't have to to express necessity, obligation, prohibition, and freedom from obligation accurately.
lesson_content_markdown:
# Necessity vs. Obligation

## The Most Dangerous Modal Pair in Academic English

The contrast between **mustn't** and **don't have to** is one of the most consequential modal distinctions in English. Confusing them can reverse the meaning of an academic claim entirely.

---

## The Core Distinctions

| Modal | Meaning | Academic Example |
|---|---|---|
| **must** | Strong internal necessity; logical deduction | *All data **must** be anonymised before analysis.* |
| **have to** | External obligation (rule, law, institution) | *Students **have to** submit a signed ethics form.* |
| **need to** | Practical necessity | *Researchers **need to** obtain written consent.* |
| **mustn't** | **Prohibition** — it is forbidden | *Participants **mustn't** be identified in the published findings.* |
| **don't have to** | **Absence of obligation** — it is not required | *Supervisors **don't have to** attend every session.* |

---

## The Critical Contrast

❌ *Participants mustn't sign a consent form* = Participants are **forbidden** from signing.
✅ *Participants **don't have to** sign a consent form* = Signing is not required.

The difference is not stylistic — it is a complete **reversal of meaning**.

---

## Common Form Errors

❌ *Researchers will must obtain approval* → ✅ *Researchers **must** obtain approval* (modals cannot be chained)
❌ *Students don't needs to register* → ✅ *Students **don't need to** register* (base form after auxiliary)
❌ *Supervisors ought to reviewed drafts* → ✅ *Supervisors **ought to review** drafts* (base form after *ought to*)

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: The passage below contains five modal errors. Identify each incorrect word and type the correct replacement.

activity_data.sentence: All researchers will must obtain ethical approval before beginning data collection. Graduate students don't needs to attend all research seminars; participation is optional. Lead investigators should ensures that all co-authors have reviewed the final manuscript. Supervisors ought to reviewed draft chapters within ten working days of receiving them. Participants mustn't provide informed consent; it is a mandatory legal and ethical requirement.
